response.justification: The candidate demonstrates strong full-stack and polyglot development skills with a focus on web and cloud technologies, but their direct experience with C++ in industrial automation or high-performance systems is limited. Their background in secure development, event-driven architectures, and mentorship aligns partially with the role, though the lack of recent C++ work and industrial cybersecurity expertise is a notable gap.
strengths: Extensive experience with event-driven architectures, microservices, and scalable systems (e.g., GISCollective, Dunnhumby media).
Proven leadership in mentoring, technical documentation, and agile practices (e.g., workshops, Refactoring Dojos).
Diverse technical toolkit including secure coding, multi-threading (DLang), and cloud technologies (AWS, Kubernetes).
concerns: Limited recent hands-on experience with C++ (last noted in 2015 at National Instruments).
No explicit mention of industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443) or Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL).
Primary expertise lies in web/mobile stacks (TypeScript, Node.js, React) rather than embedded/industrial automation.
